articles.headline: NRI Investors in India Increase by 5 Lakh in Seven Years
articles.summary: The number of active Non-Resident Indian (NRI) accounts has grown from 3.14 lakh in FY 2018-19 to over 9.42 lakh by June 2026. The total value of equity holdings in these accounts has surged from 1.5 lakh crore to 5.6 lakh crore rupees. This growth is attributed to government efforts and the Reserve Bank of India's move to simplify investment pathways and increase individual investment limits.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Quashes 23-Year-Old Case Against Standard Chartered Bank
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has canceled a long-pending criminal proceeding against Standard Chartered Bank and one of its officials. The case, dating back to 1991-92, related to alleged FERA violations. The bench ruled that the Enforcement Directorate failed to follow mandatory procedures, including providing the accused an opportunity to explain the transactions before filing a criminal complaint.

articles.headline: Ship Movement in Hormuz Strait Drops Due to West Asia Conflict
articles.summary: Maritime traffic through the critical Hormuz Strait has decreased due to security concerns following new attacks in the region. Between July 17 and 19, daily transit averaged 13 ships, a significant drop from previous weeks. The Ministry of Ports, Shipping, and Waterways informed Parliament that India has successfully navigated 59 vessels through the strait since March 1, 2026.

articles.headline: Bajaj Auto Net Profit Rises 46% to 3,225 Crore Rupees
articles.summary: Vehicle manufacturer Bajaj Auto reported a 46% year-on-year increase in consolidated net profit for the first quarter of FY27, reaching 3,225.63 crore rupees. Revenue grew 65% to 21,688.8 crore rupees, driven by record vehicle sales, better realisations, and strong performance in both domestic and export markets. The company plans to increase its annual production capacity to 90 lakh units.

articles.headline: Arvind Fashions Net Profit Jumps 57% in First Quarter
articles.summary: Apparel major Arvind Fashions Limited (AFL) reported a 57% surge in consolidated net profit to 27.61 crore rupees for the first quarter ending June 2026. Operational income increased to 1,278.50 crore rupees from 1,107.31 crore in the same period last year. The results reflect strong brand performance and improved operational efficiencies.

articles.headline: Bluestone Jewellery Records 5.96 Crore Rupee Profit in Q1
articles.summary: Bluestone Jewellery and Lifestyle Limited reported a consolidated net profit of 5.96 crore rupees for the first quarter of FY27. This marks a recovery from a net loss of 34.74 crore rupees in the same quarter of the previous fiscal year. Total income for the Bengaluru-based company rose by 49% to 751.81 crore rupees.

articles.headline: JSW Infrastructure Net Profit Declines 8.2% Due to Higher Expenses
articles.summary: JSW Infrastructure reported an 8.2% drop in consolidated net profit for the June quarter, falling to 357.60 crore rupees. Despite a rise in total income to 1,501.63 crore rupees, increased operational expenses led to the profit decline. The port operator handled 3.1 crore tonnes of cargo during the April-June period, up 6% year-on-year.

articles.headline: Adani Energy Solutions Profit Doubles to 1,236 Crore Rupees
articles.summary: Adani Energy Solutions reported a consolidated net profit of 1,236.56 crore rupees for the April-June quarter, more than double the 538.94 crore rupees recorded a year ago. Total income jumped to 9,852.20 crore rupees from 7,025.49 crore. Revenue growth was driven by its transmission and distribution businesses, which saw significant gains.

articles.headline: Bandhan Bank First Quarter Profit Grows 35% to 502 Crore
articles.summary: Private lender Bandhan Bank reported a 35% year-on-year increase in net profit to 502 crore rupees for the first quarter of FY27. Net Interest Income (NII) grew by 5.9% to 2,921 crore rupees. The bank's total advances reached 1,55,555 crore rupees as of June 2026, representing an annual growth of 16.4%.

articles.headline: Cognizant Signs $500 Million Healthcare Deal With Santene
articles.summary: IT services giant Cognizant has secured a multi-year deal worth approximately $500 million with US-based healthcare insurance firm Santene. Cognizant will manage medical billing, claims settlement, and customer call center operations for the company. This deal is part of CEO Ravi Kumar S's strategy to win large-scale contracts in the healthcare vertical.

articles.headline: Lupin Spinoffs Two Oncology Programs Into New Subsidiary Kavari Therapeutics
articles.summary: Mumbai-based pharmaceutical company Lupin has separated two of its oncology clinical programs into a wholly-owned US subsidiary, Kavari Therapeutics. The programs focus on cancer research for lung, pancreatic, and bladder tumors. Kavari will seek external funding for global clinical trials while maintaining Lupin's significant equity stake.

articles.headline: Cashfree Payments Waives Fees for New Merchants Before Festive Season
articles.summary: Bengaluru-based fintech Cashfree Payments is waiving payment gateway fees for the first 20 lakh rupees of gross business value for new merchants. The move aims to double its base of small and medium businesses (SMBs) to 20 lakh by the end of FY27. Approximately 25,000 new companies sign up for the platform monthly.

articles.headline: Contributions to GitHub Sponsors Cross $100 Million Milestone
articles.summary: GitHub reported that total contributions through its GitHub Sponsors platform have exceeded $100 million. Launched in 2019, the platform allows individuals and organizations to financially support open-source software developers. It has facilitated support for over 70,000 maintainers from 280,000 sponsors, including Fortune 500 companies.

articles.headline: Competition Commission Dismisses Anti-Competitive Complaint Against Jindal Stainless
articles.summary: The Competition Commission of India (CCI) has dismissed allegations that Jindal Stainless Limited entered into anti-competitive agreements with Indonesian companies and nickel producers. The complaint was filed by an anonymous Indian stainless steel manufacturer. The CCI found no evidence of conduct that harmed domestic market competition.

articles.headline: Foreign Portfolio Investors Return to Indian Markets in July
articles.summary: Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs) have become net buyers in the Indian equity market during the first half of July 2026, investing 15,559 crore rupees. They focused on sectors like consumer services, metals, mining, and healthcare. This shift follows massive sell-offs in Taiwan and South Korean memory chip companies, making India an attractive alternative.

articles.headline: Mutual Funds Prepare to Launch New Balanced Hybrid Category Schemes
articles.summary: Following regulatory changes and tax reforms, mutual fund houses including SBI, Kotak, and ICICI Prudential are preparing to launch schemes in the 'Balanced Hybrid' category. These funds must maintain equity and debt allocation within a strict 40-60% range. Recent changes in capital gains tax have reduced the tax disadvantage previously associated with this category.

articles.headline: SBI Chairman States No Plans to Sell Stake in SBI Funds
articles.summary: State Bank of India (SBI) Chairman C.S. Setty clarified that the bank has no immediate plans to reduce its stake in its asset management subsidiary, SBI Funds Management. Following the listing of the AMC, Setty emphasized that the current priority is maintaining leadership and growth. SBI currently holds about 55.46% stake in the joint venture with French firm Amundi.

articles.headline: Indian Rupee Stabilizes After Five-Day Decline Against US Dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee's five-day losing streak ended on Tuesday, closing at 96.24 per dollar compared to Monday's 96.45. The recovery was supported by falling crude oil prices and increased foreign exchange inflows under RBI's recent measures. Despite the slight recovery, the rupee remains approximately 10% lower compared to last year's values.

articles.headline: Havells India Faces Margin Pressure Despite Strong Revenue Growth
articles.summary: Consumer electrical major Havells India reported strong revenue growth of 19.7% in the June quarter, led by its cable and wire segment. however, operating margins fell to 7.3% from 9.6% due to high raw material costs and increased marketing spend for its Lloyd brand. Analysts expect margin recovery in the second half of the fiscal year as prices stabilize.

articles.headline: Indian Stock Markets Close Lower for Second Consecutive Session
articles.summary: Indian benchmark indices fell for the second straight day on Tuesday. The BSE Sensex dropped 238.41 points (0.31%) to close at 77,470.11, while the NSE Nifty fell 50.80 points to 24,187.70. Factors contributing to the decline include rising US-Iran tensions, higher crude oil prices, and sustained selling pressure in HDFC Bank shares.

articles.headline: Aditya Birla Sun Life AMC Profit Increases 12% in Q1
articles.summary: Aditya Birla Sun Life AMC reported a 12% increase in net profit to 309 crore rupees for the first quarter of FY27. A 38% rise in other income, which reached 162 crore rupees, contributed significantly to the bottom line. The company managed average assets under management (AUM) of 4.3 lakh crore rupees during the quarter.

articles.headline: IIM Mumbai Launches One-Year Executive MBA in Wealth Management
articles.summary: The Indian Institute of Management (IIM) Mumbai, in collaboration with Neo Wealth and Asset Management, has launched a one-year Executive MBA program specialized in Wealth Management. Targeted at working professionals, the course is designed to meet the evolving needs of India's fast-growing wealth management sector and foster industry-academia collaboration.

articles.headline: IMF Warns of Risks to India GDP Growth from Weak Monsoon
articles.summary: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has lowered India's GDP growth forecast for 2026-27 by 10 basis points to 6.4%. Key risks cited include rising petroleum prices due to the Middle East conflict and the impact of El Nino on the monsoon season. However, the IMF raised the 2027-28 forecast to 6.7%, expecting a recovery after the initial slowdown.

articles.headline: RBI Discounted Swaps Expected to Attract $60-70 Billion Inflows
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's concessional swap facilities for FCNR(B) deposits and external borrowings have already attracted $20.72 billion as of July 17. Experts now predict total inflows could reach $60-70 billion by the September 30 deadline. These measures aim to strengthen foreign exchange reserves amid global uncertainty and manage the current account deficit.

articles.headline: Government Denies Proposal to Reintroduce Ethanol-Free Petrol Sales
articles.summary: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas informed Parliament that there is no proposal to revert to selling ethanol-free petrol or petrol with only 10% ethanol. Minister of State Suresh Gopi stated that E20 petrol (20% ethanol blend) has been scientifically validated and adopted by the vehicle industry. Over 1 lakh petrol pumps currently offer various ethanol blends.

articles.headline: India Fertilizer Exports Increase 52% Over Three Years
articles.summary: India's fertilizer exports have grown by approximately 52% between FY 2023-24 and FY 2025-26. While India remains a major importer, its urea and DAP exports to neighboring countries like Sri Lanka and Nepal, as well as to Brazil, have seen significant increases. Sri Lanka's imports from India rose from 816 tonnes to 33,000 tonnes in this period.

articles.headline: PLI Schemes Generate 14.5 Lakh Jobs Across 14 Key Sectors
articles.summary: The Ministry of Commerce and Industry reported that Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes have generated 14.5 lakh jobs and attracted 2.4 lakh crore rupees in investment through March 2026. High-capacity solar PV modules saw the highest investment at 64,873 crore rupees. The schemes have significantly boosted exports, reaching 15.2 lakh crore in FY 2025-26.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Committee Calls for Increased Transparency in Competition Commission Penalties
articles.summary: A parliamentary panel has recommended that the Competition Commission of India (CCI) provide clear methodologies for calculating penalties to ensure fairness and transparency. The committee emphasized the need to protect MSMEs and startups while aligning Indian laws with global standards. The CCI informed the panel it has settled 1,237 out of 1,375 anti-competitive cases received.

articles.headline: 16th Finance Commission Faces Challenges in Balancing Center-State Relations
articles.summary: M. Govinda Rao discusses the critical role of the 16th Finance Commission in managing fiscal transfers between the Center and States. Concerns are raised over the potential discontinuation of revenue deficit grants and the heavy weighting (42.5%) given to income distance. The inclusion of state-wise GDP contribution as a 10% weight factor is also debated for its impact on fiscal equality.

articles.headline: RBI Foreign Exchange Swaps Show Better Than Expected Results
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's concessional swap measures have attracted $20.72 billion in just over a month, exceeding market expectations. Most of this ($17.41 billion) came from FCNR(B) deposits. The editorial notes that while these inflows provide a buffer against global volatility and high oil prices, they should not be used merely to defend the rupee's exchange rate.

articles.headline: Jal Jeevan 2.0 Focuses on Service Delivery and Water Quality Standards
articles.summary: Jal Jeevan Mission 2.0 has been extended to December 2028 with a total allocation of 8.69 lakh crore rupees. The updated program shifts focus from simple tap installations to ensuring consistent water quality and quantity. Challenges remain, including data inaccuracies regarding active connections and the depletion of groundwater sources as reported by NASA and NITI Aayog.

articles.headline: Maharashtra Considers Blockchain Law for Real Estate Tokenization
articles.summary: The Maharashtra government is considering the 'DELTA' Act to enable blockchain-based tokenization of immovable property. Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis stated the law aims to provide legal security for digital land transactions and help the state reach its $1 trillion economy goal by 2030. Tokenization would allow property owners to leverage the true value of their assets efficiently.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Issues Notice to Center Regarding Assam NRC Appeals
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has sought a response from the Central Government on a petition regarding the appeal process for those excluded from the final Assam NRC list. The petition by Assam State Jamiat Ulama claims the process is stalled, leaving excluded individuals in distress. The bench noted that while legal provisions for appeals exist, no action is being taken.

articles.headline: Over 3 Lakh People Affected as Assam Floods Hit 11 Districts
articles.summary: Floods in Assam have claimed one more life, bringing the total death toll to 11. Over 3.10 lakh people across 11 districts remain affected as major rivers, including the Brahmaputra, flow above danger levels. Rescue teams from the NDRF, SDRF, and the Army are deployed for relief operations. Agriculture and property damage is reported to be extensive.

articles.headline: Welfare Programs Face Challenges in Managing Beneficiary Databases and Exclusions
articles.summary: A study highlights that cash transfer schemes in Maharashtra and Odisha have improved beneficiary savings and household spending. However, governments are increasingly cleaning databases using AI and e-KYC, leading to massive exclusions. For instance, Maharashtra recently removed 92 lakh beneficiaries from a women's welfare scheme citing tax-paying or government employee status.

articles.headline: Leaking NEET Papers is a Great Sin Says PM Narendra Modi
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi broke his silence on the NEET paper leak controversy, calling it a 'great sin.' During a meeting with NDA MPs, he assured that strict action would be taken against those involved. Meanwhile, opposition protests intensified near the PM's residence, with Congress leaders Rahul and Priyanka Gandhi being detained during a 'Parliament March.'

articles.headline: Mamata Banerjee Invites Congress and CPM to Join INDIA Alliance
articles.summary: TMC Supremo Mamata Banerjee has extended an olive branch to the Congress and CPIM in West Bengal, inviting them to form a united front against the BJP under the INDIA alliance. During a 'Martyr's Day' rally in Kolkata, she emphasized the need for national opposition unity. The move follows years of local rivalry between these parties in the state.

articles.headline: NSA Ajit Doval Meets Saudi Foreign Minister to Discuss Energy Security
articles.summary: National Security Advisor Ajit Doval met with Saudi Foreign Minister Prince Faisal bin Farhan in Riyadh to discuss strengthening strategic partnerships. The talks focused on ensuring 'energy security' and 'maritime trade route safety' amid heightened tensions in West Asia. This is Doval's third visit to the Kingdom this year, highlighting the deep tactical ties between the nations.

articles.headline: Aviation Sector Faces Disruption as Pilots Seek Suspension of UAE Flights
articles.summary: The Airlines Pilots Association of India has requested the DGCA to immediately suspend flights to the UAE and other Gulf regions due to security risks from the US-Iran conflict. Indian carriers are heavily dependent on the UAE market, which accounts for 26.2% of total international traffic. A suspension would force airlines to ground planes, further impacting a sector still recovering.

articles.headline: Woman, 2 daughters killed in South Delhi house fire
articles.summary: A 40-year-old woman and her two daughters, aged 8 and 12, died in a fire at their third-floor flat in BK Dutt Colony near Lodhi Colony. The fire broke out on Tuesday morning; three fire tenders were used to control the blaze. The cause of the fire is currently under investigation by Delhi Police and fire officials.

articles.headline: Prime Minister Modi stresses action over paper leaks, pushes youth outreach
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi addressed the NDA parliamentary party, calling for stringent action against those involved in exam paper leaks. He urged MPs to reach out to the youth and dispel fears. Modi emphasized the government’s commitment to transparency and ensuring that the futures of students are not compromised by the "menace" of irregular exam practices.

articles.headline: Oil prices at 5-week high on Iran strikes, Houthi threats
articles.summary: Global oil prices rose about 2% after US-led forces bombed targets in Yemen following Houthi attacks on commercial vessels. Tensions in the Middle East, including potential supply disruptions near the Strait of Hormuz, have pushed Brent crude to over $81.80 per barrel. An Indian national was injured in a separate attack on a Kuwait-flagged tanker near Oman.

articles.headline: Supreme Court orders demolition of illegal constructions in Meerut EWS houses
articles.summary: The Supreme Court directed the demolition of all unauthorized structures and encroachments in Economically Weaker Section (EWS) housing areas in Meerut. The court ruled that illegal constructions cannot be regularized based on government inaction. It emphasized that public land must be protected and used for its intended social welfare purposes.

articles.headline: Retired Agniveers to get 50% CAPF reservation, exam waiver
articles.summary: The Centre informed Parliament that retired Agniveers will receive a 50% reservation in recruitment for Constable (General Duty) posts in Central Armed Police Forces (CAPFs) and Assam Rifles. They will also be exempted from the written examination and physical efficiency tests, aiming to facilitate their smooth transition into second careers after military service.

articles.headline: Floods wreak havoc in Assam and Nagaland, death toll rises
articles.summary: Flash floods and landslides triggered by heavy rainfall have killed nine people in Assam and nine in Nagaland. In Assam, the Brahmaputra and its tributaries are flowing above the danger level, affecting over 300,000 people across several districts. Relief operations by NDRF and state agencies are underway to provide food and medical assistance to displaced residents.

articles.headline: Gukesh’s slump continues, on brink of exiting elite 2700 Elo club
articles.summary: Indian Grandmaster D. Gukesh suffered a second consecutive loss at the Grand Masters tournament in Norway, losing to 19-year-old M. Pranesh. The defeat has caused Gukesh’s live Elo rating to drop significantly, placing him on the verge of falling below the 2700 mark, the unofficial benchmark for "Super GMs." He now sits at the bottom of the tournament standings.

articles.headline: Delhi government plans ₹11,785 crore elevated ring road project
articles.summary: The Delhi government has proposed a ₹11,785 crore project to build a 63km elevated ring road to ease traffic congestion. However, the plan faces opposition from the central government and environmentalists due to its route passing through parts of the Yamuna bank and the Ridge area. A conflict over environmental clearances is expected as the project progresses.

articles.headline: Supreme Court asks Sonam Raghuvanshi to surrender or defend bail
articles.summary: The Supreme Court directed Sonam Raghuvanshi, accused of murdering her husband in Meghalaya in 2022, to either surrender to the state police or move a fresh bail application. The court gave her two days to decide after observing that the state's plea to cancel her bail had merit due to the seriousness of the charges and potential influence on witnesses.

articles.headline: US judge blocks Trump administration from stripping asylum seeker work permits
articles.summary: A federal judge in Boston has temporarily blocked the Trump administration from implementing a policy that would have stripped work permits from thousands of asylum seekers and immigrants under Temporary Protected Status (TPS). The ruling came in response to a lawsuit filed by civil rights groups arguing the move violated existing labor and immigration laws.

articles.headline: Mittal family-led group in talks for stake in Liverpool Football Club
articles.summary: A group led by the family of steel tycoon Lakshmi Mittal is reportedly in discussions to acquire a minority stake in Liverpool FC. The deal would value the Premier League club at over $6 billion. The investment would provide Fenway Sports Group with fresh capital for infrastructure and player acquisitions while expanding the club's global brand presence in India.

articles.headline: Ram Janmabhoomi Trust to meet to appoint new CEO
articles.summary: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust will hold two crucial meetings in Ayodhya to appoint a new Chief Executive Officer (CEO) and fill three vacant trustee positions. The appointments come as the trust focuses on the next phase of construction and managing the increasing influx of devotees at the temple complex.

articles.headline: AI to drive planning and fraud detection under MNREGA scheme
articles.summary: The Ministry of Rural Development plans to integrate Artificial Intelligence (AI) into the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme (MNREGA). AI will be used to analyze satellite imagery for project planning, monitor work progress, and detect fraudulent claims. The move aims to improve transparency and efficiency in the world's largest social welfare program.

articles.headline: Black Sea becomes key battleground in Russia-Ukraine war
articles.summary: The Black Sea has emerged as a critical front in the ongoing conflict between Russia and Ukraine. Both sides have intensified attacks on grain ports, naval vessels, and logistics networks. Recent missile strikes on Ukraine's Odesa port and Russian naval bases in Crimea highlight the strategic importance of the region for global food security and military operations.

articles.headline: Police seek AI chat logs to aid Bengaluru triple murder probe
articles.summary: Bengaluru police have requested chat logs from an AI company to assist in the investigation of a triple murder case in the city. Investigators believe one of the suspects used an AI chatbot to research methods for disposing of bodies and cleaning crime scenes. The data is expected to provide crucial evidence regarding the premeditated nature of the crimes.

articles.headline: Two brothers booked for exhuming mother’s body in Davangere
articles.summary: Police in Davangere have booked two brothers for illegally exhuming their mother's body from a burial site without official permission. The brothers reportedly dug up the body and moved it to a private location, claiming they wanted to perform final rites according to their beliefs. They face charges for desecrating a grave and violating public health protocols.

articles.headline: Karnataka DyCM defends decision to distribute free Permanent Resident Certificates
articles.summary: Karnataka Deputy CM D.K. Shivakumar defended the government's plan to issue free Permanent Resident Certificates (PRCs) to eligible students in the state's border regions. He stated the move is intended to facilitate access to education and government jobs for residents in linguistically sensitive areas. The decision had faced criticism from some political quarters over eligibility criteria.

articles.headline: BBMP official assaulted during anti-encroachment drive in Bengaluru
articles.summary: An official from the Bruhat Bengaluru Mahanagara Palike (BBMP) was allegedly assaulted by a group of local residents during an anti-encroachment drive in the city. The incident occurred as teams were clearing illegal structures blocking a major drain. Police have registered a case against four individuals and are providing additional security for ongoing demolition works.

articles.headline: Survey finds 9 out of 10 schools in border taluks dilapidated
articles.summary: A survey conducted by a committee appointed by the Karnataka government revealed that nearly 90% of schools in the state's border taluks are in a dilapidated condition. The report highlighted poor infrastructure, lack of basic amenities, and teacher shortages. It recommended an immediate allocation of ₹500 crore for the renovation and upgrade of these educational facilities.

articles.headline: Centre increasingly afraid of peaceful protests: Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah
articles.summary: Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah criticized the central government, alleging it is "increasingly afraid" of peaceful democratic protests. His comments followed the detention of opposition leaders during a protest in Delhi over exam leaks. He accused the Centre of using police force and tear gas to suppress the voices of students and political activists raising legitimate concerns.

articles.headline: Row over Kaleshwaram project deepens amid drought in Telangana
articles.summary: Tensions between the ruling Congress and opposition BRS in Telangana have escalated over the operation of the Kaleshwaram Lift Irrigation Scheme during a dry spell. The government argues that pumping water is currently inadvisable due to structural issues, while the BRS claims the project is being neglected to politically discredit the previous administration’s landmark work.

articles.headline: Supreme Court stops government supervision of Kerala Waqf Board
articles.summary: The Supreme Court stayed a Kerala High Court order that had permitted the state government to supervise the Kerala Waqf Board. The apex court ruled that the board should continue to function as a statutory body without direct government oversight, pending further hearing. The petition argued that state intervention interfered with the independent administration of religious properties.

articles.headline: Doctors rule out poison in trainee IPS officer's suicide attempt
articles.summary: Medical professionals treating a trainee IPS officer in Hyderabad have ruled out the presence of poison in his system following a suspected suicide attempt. The officer was hospitalized after consuming an excessive amount of alcohol. While initial reports suggested self-harm, doctors stated his condition was solely due to severe alcohol intoxication. He remains under observation.

articles.headline: No formal bid to shift Parandur airport site: Civil Aviation Ministry
articles.summary: The Union Ministry of Civil Aviation stated it has received no formal request from the Tamil Nadu government to shift the proposed site for the Parandur greenfield airport. This clarifies rumors about potential relocation due to local protests and environmental concerns. The project, estimated at ₹20,000 crore, is planned to serve as Chennai's second major airport.

articles.headline: India’s youngest municipal chairperson voted out in Kerala
articles.summary: Divya Binoy, who became India's youngest municipal chairperson at age 21, was voted out of her position in a no-confidence motion in Kerala's Kottayam district. The motion was supported by the opposition UDF and some rebel members of her own party. She was leading the Palai Municipality and has stated she will continue to serve as a ward counselor.

articles.headline: Four arrested for kidnapping scrap metal businessman in Chennai
articles.summary: Chennai police arrested four men for allegedly kidnapping a 58-year-old scrap metal businessman at gunpoint and demanding a ₹50 lakh ransom. The victim was abducted from near his warehouse. Police tracked the kidnappers using CCTV footage and rescued the businessman within 24 hours. A search is underway for two other accomplices who are currently at large.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu Assembly Speaker clarifies rules for arrest of sitting MLA
articles.summary: TN Assembly Speaker M. Appavu clarified the protocol for arresting a sitting MLA, stating that the Speaker's prior permission is not required but the office must be informed immediately after the arrest. This statement followed concerns raised by the DMK regarding the potential arrest of one of its members. The Speaker emphasized adherence to legislative rules and procedures.

articles.headline: IIT Roorkee tells students and staff not to engage in protests
articles.summary: The Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Roorkee issued an advisory to its students and staff, instructing them not to participate in any political protests or demonstrations. The institute cited its code of conduct, which prohibits activities that could disrupt academic atmosphere or bring disrepute to the institution. The move follows recent student-led rallies on campus.

articles.headline: Police thwart farmers' march towards Delhi at Ghaziabad border
articles.summary: Delhi and Uttar Pradesh police blocked a march by thousands of farmers heading toward the national capital. The farmers, demanding higher crop prices and debt waivers, were stopped at the Ghaziabad border using barricades and water cannons. Representatives of the farmer unions met with government officials to discuss their grievances, but the standoff continues.

articles.headline: Two TMC factions clash at rallies in West Bengal, exchange barbs
articles.summary: Clashes erupted between two rival factions of the Trinamool Congress (TMC) during parallel rallies held in West Bengal. Several supporters were injured as groups exchanged heated arguments and physical blows over local leadership disputes. Senior party leaders have called for restraint and launched an internal inquiry into the incident to restore discipline within the ranks.

articles.headline: Centre confirms proxy rule in many Panchayats across India
articles.summary: The Union government informed Parliament that "proxy" governance remains a reality in several village panchayats, where male relatives often exercise the power of elected women representatives. Despite mandatory reservation for women, reports indicate that "Sarpanch-patis" continue to manage administrative tasks. The Centre plans to launch new training programs to empower women leaders.

articles.headline: CJI questions support for DAMEPL despite Supreme Court defeat
articles.summary: Chief Justice of India D.Y. Chandrachud questioned why some continue to support the Delhi Airport Metro Express Private Ltd (DAMEPL) despite its recent legal defeat. The SC had earlier set aside an arbitral award in favor of DAMEPL, a Reliance Infrastructure subsidiary, citing it as "patently illegal." The CJI emphasized that judicial decisions must be respected to maintain the rule of law.

articles.headline: Judge recuses himself from hearing Gadling's bail plea
articles.summary: A judge in the Bombay High Court has recused himself from hearing the bail application of activist Surendra Gadling, an accused in the Elgar Parishad-Bhima Koregaon case. The judge did not provide a specific reason for his withdrawal from the bench. The matter will now be listed before another bench. Gadling has been in custody since June 2018.

articles.headline: UAPA accused flagged by facial recognition system in J&K, held
articles.summary: An individual wanted in a case under the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act was apprehended in Jammu & Kashmir after being flagged by an AI-powered facial recognition system. The system, deployed at a high-security checkpoint, identified the suspect from a database of known offenders. Police stated that the technology has become a vital tool in tracking fugitives in the region.

articles.headline: Father’s funeral: Court grants interim bail to Parliament breach accused
articles.summary: A Delhi court granted interim bail to Sagar Sharma, one of the accused in the 2023 Parliament security breach case, to attend his father's last rites. The bail was granted for a period of ten days on humanitarian grounds. Sharma was directed to remain within the jurisdiction of the court and surrender immediately after the bail period expires.

articles.headline: Bangladesh's pick for mission spokespersons in India raises eyebrows
articles.summary: The Bangladesh government's decision to appoint two journalists as spokespersons for its missions in New Delhi and Kolkata has caused controversy. Critics point out that both individuals have previously made derogatory social media posts about India and its government. Diplomatic observers suggest these appointments could complicate the ongoing efforts to rebuild bilateral ties.

articles.headline: Jaishankar to meet Quad counterparts in the Philippines this week
articles.summary: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar will travel to Manila to meet with foreign ministers from the US, Australia, and Japan for a Quad grouping meeting. The discussions will focus on regional security, maritime cooperation in the Indo-Pacific, and emerging technology partnerships. This meeting precedes the annual Quad Leaders' Summit scheduled for later this year.

articles.headline: Anti-submarine craft Malvan to join Indian Navy today
articles.summary: The Indian Navy is set to commission its second indigenously built Mahe-class anti-submarine warfare shallow water craft, INS Malvan, in Kochi. The vessel, with over 80% local content, is designed to perform sub-surface surveillance and search operations in coastal waters. It is equipped with advanced sonar systems and lightweight torpedoes to counter submarine threats.

articles.headline: India to play key role in COP-31: UNFCCC executive secretary
articles.summary: The executive secretary of the UNFCCC, Simon Stiell, stated that India is poised to play a crucial role in the upcoming COP-31 climate summit. During his visit to Delhi, Stiell praised India's rapid expansion of renewable energy and its commitment to green transition goals. He emphasized that India's leadership is vital for achieving global climate targets.

articles.headline: Allahabad HC delivers split verdict on 'bulldozer justice' case
articles.summary: A two-judge bench of the Allahabad High Court delivered a split verdict in a case challenging the demolition of houses without prior notice. One judge ruled that the demolitions were illegal and violated due process, while the other held that the state acted within its rights to clear unauthorized structures. The matter has been referred to a larger bench for a final decision.

articles.headline: Donald Trump weighs new Iran ceasefire or large-scale airstrikes
articles.summary: US President-elect Donald Trump is reportedly considering two drastically different strategies for Iran: a new 10-day ceasefire to reopen diplomatic channels or launching massive airstrikes against military targets. Axios reports that Trump's advisors are divided on the approach, with some advocating for "maximum pressure" and others favoring a negotiated cooling-off period.

articles.headline: Tanker with Indian crew was hit by US while drifting
articles.summary: New details have emerged regarding the oil tanker MT Settebello, which was hit by a US airstrike in the Red Sea. The vessel, carrying an Indian crew, was reportedly drifting after engine failure and was not actively involved in any military activity. All 23 Indian crew members are safe. The US military is investigating why the ship was targeted.

articles.headline: Spousal work curbs led to drop in Indian student applications: Canada
articles.summary: Canada's immigration authorities reported a significant drop in student visa applications from India following new restrictions on work permits for spouses of international students. The policy change, aimed at managing temporary resident numbers, has made Canada less attractive for those seeking to relocate with families. Indian students traditionally make up the largest group of applicants.

articles.headline: Anthropic to pay $1.5 billion for copyright settlement
articles.summary: AI startup Anthropic has agreed to a $1.5 billion settlement with a group of authors and publishers over allegations that it used copyrighted books to train its AI models without permission. The settlement, one of the largest of its kind, includes licensing fees for future use of literary works. This sets a major precedent for how AI companies handle intellectual property.

articles.headline: Lebanese army begins deploying in southern town after Israel deal
articles.summary: The Lebanese army started moving into towns in southern Lebanon as part of a ceasefire agreement aimed at ending hostilities with Israel. The deployment is intended to establish a buffer zone and ensure that non-state armed groups do not operate near the border. UN peacekeepers are monitoring the situation to prevent any violations of the peace deal.

articles.headline: Spain moves to ban smoking in terraces, beaches and parks
articles.summary: The Spanish government has proposed a new law to ban smoking in outdoor public spaces, including restaurant terraces, beaches, and parks. The health ministry stated the ban is necessary to reduce second-hand smoke exposure and promote public health. The proposal has faced opposition from the hospitality sector, which fears it will impact tourism and local businesses.

articles.headline: UN: Scam losses in Asian regions up to $114 billion in 2025
articles.summary: A United Nations report revealed that transnational online scams in Southeast Asia resulted in losses of up to $114 billion in 2025. The report highlighted how organized crime syndicates use human trafficking victims to operate scam centers. Most victims are lured with fake job offers and then forced to conduct financial fraud targeting people globally.

articles.headline: Bill Gates foundation to pay $30 million for external review
articles.summary: The Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation has agreed to pay $30 million to settle allegations related to its past management and governance. The funds will go toward an independent external review of the foundation's operations. The move comes after concerns were raised about the foundation's influence on global health policy and its internal decision-making processes.

articles.headline: TVS, Bajaj Auto fast lane after Q1 profit surge
articles.summary: Indian two-wheeler majors TVS Motor and Bajaj Auto reported significant profit growth in the first quarter of the fiscal year. TVS saw a 42% rise in net profit, while Bajaj Auto's profit grew by 25%. Both companies cited strong demand in the domestic market and an increase in electric vehicle sales as key drivers of their financial performance.

articles.headline: Rupee has no target exchange rate, says Union government
articles.summary: The Union government stated that the Indian rupee is market-determined and does not have a specific target exchange rate against the US dollar. Officials emphasized that the RBI intervenes only to manage excessive volatility. This statement follows the rupee hitting a record low of 84.15 against the dollar, driven by global economic uncertainties and rising crude oil prices.

articles.headline: Novo sues Lilly in US over obesity drug ads
articles.summary: Pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk has filed a lawsuit against Eli Lilly in the US, alleging that advertisements for Lilly's new obesity drug are misleading. Novo claims the ads make false comparisons between the two companies' products. The legal battle highlights the intense competition in the rapidly growing market for weight-loss medications.

articles.headline: Lower reservoir levels may hit crops, power generation: Central Water Commission
articles.summary: The Central Water Commission (CWC) warned that water levels in India's major reservoirs are significantly lower than average for this time of year. This could impact agricultural productivity for the Kharif crop and reduce hydroelectric power generation capacity. The commission urged states to manage water resources efficiently until the monsoon provides adequate replenishment.

articles.headline: India's coking coal import bill falls 39% in 4 years
articles.summary: India's import bill for coking coal has dropped by 39% over the last four years, according to government data. The decline is attributed to a shift toward domestic production and sourcing from a wider range of international suppliers. This move is part of the government's strategy to reduce import dependency and lower the cost of steel production.

articles.headline: CWG: Recent records raise India's hopes in athletics
articles.summary: A series of national records set by Indian athletes in recent months has bolstered hopes for a strong performance at the upcoming Commonwealth Games. High expectations are placed on the track and field contingent, with several athletes currently ranked among the top in the world in their respective events. Training camps are being held across India and Europe to finalize preparations.

articles.headline: Lawn bowls stars bear weight of expectations after 2022 success
articles.summary: Following their historic gold medal win at the 2022 Commonwealth Games, the Indian lawn bowls team faces high expectations for the next edition. The team has been undergoing intensive training to maintain its competitive edge. Players stated that while the pressure is high, the increased recognition of the sport in India has provided significant motivation.

articles.headline: Sakshi Chaudhary gears up for Commonwealth Games after beating Nikhat Zareen
articles.summary: Boxer Sakshi Chaudhary is preparing for the Commonwealth Games after a major upset victory over world champion Nikhat Zareen in the national selection trials. Chaudhary's performance has earned her a spot in the Indian contingent. She expressed confidence in her preparation and aims to secure a podium finish in the highly competitive bantamweight category.

articles.headline: Indian hockey squad named for next month's World Cup
articles.summary: Hockey India has announced a 20-member squad for the upcoming Men's Hockey World Cup. The team, led by experienced players, includes several young talents who have impressed in recent international tournaments. The squad will undergo a final preparatory camp in Bengaluru before departing for the tournament venue.

articles.headline: Bowlers face entire match suspension for deliberate no balls: BCCI
articles.summary: The BCCI has introduced a new rule stating that any bowler found deliberately bowling no balls will face an immediate suspension for the remainder of the match. The move is part of an effort to curb unfair play and maintain the integrity of domestic cricket. The rule will be implemented across all BCCI-sanctioned tournaments starting next season.

articles.headline: Setback for LIV Golf as Asian Tour joins European, PGA Tours
articles.summary: In a significant blow to LIV Golf, the Asian Tour has announced a new partnership with the European Tour and the PGA Tour. This move effectively isolates the LIV Golf circuit and restricts its players' access to major international rankings. The partnership aims to provide a more unified global pathway for professional golfers.

articles.headline: World Cup champions Spain get grand welcome in Madrid
articles.summary: The Spanish national football team received a hero's welcome in Madrid after winning the FIFA World Cup. Thousands of fans lined the streets for a victory parade through the city. The team met with King Felipe VI and Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez at the royal palace, celebrating their first global title in 16 years.

articles.headline: Lionel Messi returns to Argentina for rest before World Cup
articles.summary: Lionel Messi has arrived in Argentina for a short break before joining the national squad's final preparations for the World Cup. Fans gathered at the airport to catch a glimpse of the superstar. Messi is expected to spend time with his family in Rosario before flying back to the training camp in Europe.

articles.headline: Won't spare paper leakers, making exams foolproof: PM Modi
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi stated the government is working to overhaul the examination system following recent paper leak scandals. Addressing the NDA parliamentary party, he emphasized that the youth's future is a top priority and promised stringent punishment for those involved in irregularities. New laws have been enacted to curb organized crime in public examinations.

articles.headline: Haryana barricades borders to stop farmers' Delhi march
articles.summary: Haryana Police have erected multi-layer barricades and deployed heavy security forces at border points to prevent farmers from marching toward New Delhi. Mirroring the 'Delhi Chalo-2' protests from earlier this year, farmers from Punjab and Haryana are demanding legal guarantees for Minimum Support Prices (MSP) and loan waivers as they attempt to reach the capital.

articles.headline: Detaining man on way to protest is gundagardi: High Court
articles.summary: The Uttarakhand High Court strongly criticized the police for detaining an activist who was traveling to join a protest in New Delhi. The bench described the police action as 'sheer gundagardi' (thuggery) and emphasized that every citizen has the constitutional right to protest peacefully without harassment from the state or national security agencies.

articles.headline: BDA open house resolves over 2,000 pending complaints
articles.summary: The Bangalore Development Authority's (BDA) 'Open House Meeting' initiative has successfully resolved 2,206 long-pending grievances since its launch in August 2025. The program aims to streamline land and property-related issues, including the issuance of allotment letters and site updates, by providing citizens direct access to senior officials for faster decision-making.

articles.headline: Tejasvi Surya flags electoral roll glitches in Bengaluru
articles.summary: MP Tejasvi Surya has alleged massive irregularities in the electoral roll revision process in the Bengaluru South constituency. He claimed over 13,000 valid voters were marked as 'permanently shifted' without proper verification. The Election Commission has been asked to conduct a re-verification process to ensure no eligible voters are disenfranchised.

articles.headline: Calling classmate 'pretty' not voyeurism or stalking: HC
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court quashed a criminal case against a 20-year-old student booked for stalking after he sent an Instagram message calling a female classmate 'pretty'. Justice M Nagaprasanna ruled that while the choice of words might be inappropriate, such expressions of appreciation do not constitute a criminal offense or an attempt to outrage a woman's modesty.

articles.headline: Two KGF men rescued from cyber-slavery cartel in Myanmar
articles.summary: Two youths from Kolar Gold Fields who were trafficked to Thailand and then forced into cyber-slavery in Myanmar have been safely rescued. The victims were coerced into performing online financial scams under threat of physical torture. They were repatriated following a coordinated effort by the Cyber Command Centre and various international agencies.

articles.headline: Greater Bengaluru polls face delay despite roll extension
articles.summary: The long-awaited elections for the Bruhat Bengaluru Mahanagara Palike (BBMP) are likely to face further delays as the Supreme Court extended the deadline for the finalization of electoral rolls to December 31. This extension means the civic polls, which have been pending for nearly four years, are unlikely to be held before early 2027.

articles.headline: 46 militants surrender to state government in Manipur
articles.summary: In a boost to peace efforts in Manipur, 46 militants from various proscribed groups surrendered their arms during a 'homecoming ceremony' organized by the state government. The surrendered cadres handed over a cache of weapons, including AK rifles and grenade launchers. The Chief Minister stated that the door for dialogue remains open for those willing to give up violence.

articles.headline: Muscle loss hitting Indians in 40s; diet key factor
articles.summary: Health experts warn that sarcopenia, or premature muscle loss, is increasingly affecting Indians in their 40s. Sedentary lifestyles, protein-deficient diets, and rising diabetes rates are identified as primary causes. Doctors recommend a combination of resistance training and a balanced intake of at least 1g of protein per kg of body weight to maintain muscle mass and metabolic health.

articles.headline: Education reforms protest spread from Ladakh to London
articles.summary: Massive protests demanding educational reforms and the implementation of the 6th Schedule for Ladakh have spread across multiple Indian cities and reached international locations like London. Students and activists are rallying against alleged paper leaks and calling for greater regional autonomy. The movement has gained momentum following a hunger strike by local leaders.

articles.headline: RBI draft caps gift remittances to near relatives
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has proposed a new framework to cap gift remittances to near relatives under the Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS). The draft suggests a limit of $2,50,000 for such transfers, while also clarifying rules for repatriation and broadening the definition of relatives. The move aims to streamline foreign exchange management and curb potential misuse of the scheme.

articles.headline: K'taka sees fastest FDI rise among major Indian states
articles.summary: Karnataka recorded the highest growth in Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) inflows among major states in India, nearly doubling to $13 billion in the last fiscal year. The surge is attributed to the state's dominant position in the tech and Global Capability Centres (GCC) sectors. Other states like Maharashtra and Tamil Nadu also saw significant increases in investment.

articles.headline: IndiGo market share hits record high as AI reduces flights
articles.summary: IndiGo's domestic market share reached a record 66.3% last month as Air India reduced its flight frequencies. Data from the DGCA shows a significant consolidation in the Indian aviation sector, with IndiGo expanding its lead while other carriers struggle with operational challenges. The overall domestic passenger traffic saw a marginal growth of 2.5% year-on-year.

articles.headline: India go full strength for FIH Pro League hockey
articles.summary: Hockey India has announced a full-strength 20-member squad for the upcoming FIH Pro League season. Led by Harmanpreet Singh, the team includes veteran players like PR Sreejesh and Manpreet Singh as they prepare for the premier tournament in Europe. The selection committee highlighted the importance of these matches as a final tune-up for major upcoming international championships.

articles.headline: India's retail inflation rises to 5.69% in March
articles.summary: Retail inflation in India climbed to 5.69% in March, up from 5.09% in February, primarily driven by food and fuel prices. This marks a reversal of the downward trend seen in previous months.

articles.headline: RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 6.5%
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee decided to keep the repo rate steady at 6.5%. The committee voted 5-1 to maintain the status quo, focusing on aligning inflation with the target.

articles.headline: India's GDP grows 8.4% in Q3 FY24
articles.summary: India's economy expanded by 8.4% in the third quarter of the fiscal year 2023-24, exceeding expectations and signaling robust economic activity. This growth is attributed to strong manufacturing and construction sectors.

articles.headline: Sensex, Nifty hit record highs amid positive global cues
articles.summary: Indian stock markets, Sensex and Nifty, touched all-time highs today, driven by positive global market sentiment and strong corporate earnings. Investors are optimistic about economic growth prospects.

articles.headline: PM Modi to launch India's first underwater metro
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will inaugurate India's first underwater metro line in Kolkata. This engineering marvel will connect two crucial parts of the city under the Hooghly River.

articles.headline: New GST Council meeting to discuss rate rationalization
articles.summary: The Goods and Services Tax (GST) Council is scheduled to meet soon to discuss potential rationalization of tax rates. The agenda includes simplifying the multi-tiered tax structure.

articles.headline: India's trade deficit widens in March
articles.summary: India's trade deficit widened in March due to a surge in imports, particularly oil. Exports also showed a moderate increase, but the gap between imports and exports grew significantly.

articles.headline: Foreign investors remain cautious on Indian equities
articles.summary: Foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) have shown cautiousness in investing in Indian equities recently, citing global economic uncertainties and domestic regulatory factors. However, long-term outlook remains positive.

articles.headline: Tech giant announces new AI innovation center in Bengaluru
articles.summary: A leading global technology company has unveiled its new Artificial Intelligence innovation center in Bengaluru. The center will focus on developing advanced AI solutions for various industries.

articles.headline: Supreme Court hears arguments on electoral bonds
articles.summary: The Supreme Court continued hearing arguments regarding the electoral bonds scheme. The court is examining transparency and potential misuse of the political funding mechanism.

articles.headline: Monsoon forecast predicts normal rainfall for India
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department has predicted a normal monsoon rainfall for the country this year. This is crucial news for the agricultural sector and overall economy.

articles.headline: India to host G20 finance ministers' meeting next week
articles.summary: India is set to host the G20 finance ministers and central bank governors meeting in Gandhinagar. Discussions will focus on global economic stability and sustainable development.

articles.headline: Indian Railways to modernize tracks and signaling systems
articles.summary: Indian Railways has announced a plan to modernize its track and signaling infrastructure. This initiative aims to improve safety, speed, and efficiency of train operations.

articles.headline: New solar power project inaugurated in Rajasthan
articles.summary: A large-scale solar power project has been commissioned in Rajasthan, contributing significantly to India's renewable energy capacity. The project aims to reduce carbon emissions.

articles.headline: Automaker launches new electric vehicle model
articles.summary: An automotive manufacturer has introduced a new electric vehicle (EV) model in the Indian market. The launch aims to boost EV adoption and promote sustainable mobility.

articles.headline: Health Ministry launches new public health awareness campaign
articles.summary: The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare has launched a nationwide public health awareness campaign focusing on preventive healthcare. The campaign aims to educate citizens on healthy lifestyles.

articles.headline: Education ministry announces new skill development initiative
articles.summary: The Ministry of Education has launched a new initiative to enhance skill development among youth. The program aims to bridge the gap between education and industry requirements.

articles.headline: Cricket board reviews team's performance ahead of World Cup
articles.summary: The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) is reviewing the national team's recent performance. The focus is on identifying areas for improvement before the upcoming World Cup.

articles.headline: Scientists discover new exoplanet with potential for life
articles.summary: An international team of astronomers has announced the discovery of a new exoplanet located in the habitable zone of its star. Further studies are needed to confirm the presence of life.

articles.headline: Global stock markets show mixed performance
articles.summary: Global stock markets are exhibiting mixed performance today, with some indices trading higher on positive economic data and others lower due to geopolitical concerns.

articles.headline: US inflation data awaited with anticipation
articles.summary: Markets are keenly awaiting the release of crucial US inflation data, which could influence the Federal Reserve's interest rate policy. Investors are seeking clarity on the economic outlook.

articles.headline: European Central Bank holds interest rates steady
articles.summary: The European Central Bank has decided to keep its key interest rates unchanged, citing a stable but cautious economic outlook in the Eurozone. The bank is monitoring inflation trends.

articles.headline: China's economic growth shows signs of recovery
articles.summary: China's economy is showing signs of recovery, with recent data indicating improved industrial output and retail sales. However, challenges remain in the property sector.

articles.headline: Tech startup secures significant Series B funding
articles.summary: A promising tech startup has successfully raised substantial funding in its Series B round. The investment will fuel its expansion and product development efforts.

articles.headline: Artificial intelligence set to transform healthcare sector
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence (AI) is poised to revolutionize the healthcare sector by improving diagnostics, drug discovery, and patient care. Experts anticipate significant advancements in medical technology.

articles.headline: Renewable energy targets discussed at global summit
articles.summary: Global leaders convened at an international summit to discuss accelerating renewable energy adoption. The focus was on increasing investments and overcoming policy hurdles for a sustainable future.

articles.headline: Space agency announces mission to explore Mars' moon
articles.summary: A national space agency has announced a new mission to explore one of Mars' moons. The unmanned probe will study the moon's geological composition and potential for past life.

articles.headline: New archaeological discovery sheds light on ancient civilization
articles.summary: Archaeologists have unearthed a significant discovery that provides new insights into an ancient civilization. The findings include artifacts and structural remains dating back thousands of years.

articles.headline: Government outlines new policy for startups
articles.summary: The government has unveiled a new policy aimed at fostering innovation and growth in the startup ecosystem. The policy includes incentives and support measures for emerging businesses.

articles.headline: Global oil prices remain volatile
articles.summary: Global oil prices are experiencing volatility, influenced by supply concerns and geopolitical developments. Analysts predict continued fluctuations in the coming weeks.

articles.headline: Central bank digital currency experiments underway
articles.summary: Several central banks are actively experimenting with central bank digital currencies (CBDCs). The goal is to explore potential benefits and challenges of digital currencies for financial stability.

articles.headline: International court rules on maritime dispute
articles.summary: An international court has delivered a ruling on a long-standing maritime dispute between two nations. The decision aims to resolve territorial claims and promote regional stability.

articles.headline: Global food security challenges discussed
articles.summary: World leaders addressed global food security challenges at a summit, focusing on sustainable agriculture and equitable distribution. Climate change impacts were identified as a key concern.

articles.headline: Pharmaceutical company develops new cancer therapy
articles.summary: A pharmaceutical company has announced a breakthrough in cancer therapy with the development of a new treatment. Clinical trials have shown promising results in targeting specific cancer cells.

articles.headline: Space exploration collaboration announced
articles.summary: Multiple space agencies have announced a collaborative mission for future space exploration. The partnership aims to pool resources and expertise for ambitious scientific endeavors.

articles.headline: Urban infrastructure development projects gain momentum
articles.summary: Several urban infrastructure development projects are gaining momentum across the country. These include transportation, housing, and public utilities, aimed at improving city living.

articles.headline: Global summit on climate change adaptation
articles.summary: A global summit focused on climate change adaptation strategies was held. Discussions centered on building resilience and implementing measures to cope with climate impacts.

articles.headline: E-commerce growth continues to accelerate
articles.summary: The e-commerce sector continues to witness accelerated growth, driven by increasing online penetration and changing consumer behavior. Digital sales are expected to rise further.

articles.headline: India's agricultural output to increase
articles.summary: Projections indicate an increase in India's agricultural output this year, supported by favorable weather conditions and government policies. This is expected to boost rural income.

articles.headline: Legal reforms proposed for corporate governance
articles.summary: The government is proposing significant legal reforms to enhance corporate governance standards. The aim is to improve transparency, accountability, and investor protection.

articles.headline: World Bank forecasts strong economic growth for developing nations
articles.summary: The World Bank has forecast strong economic growth for developing nations, including India. The report highlights the resilience of emerging economies despite global challenges.

articles.headline: India ranks high in renewable energy deployment
articles.summary: India has been recognized for its significant progress in renewable energy deployment. The country ranks among the top globally for installed solar and wind power capacity.

articles.headline: Olympic committee announces new sports initiatives
articles.summary: The International Olympic Committee has announced new initiatives to promote sports participation globally. The focus is on grassroots development and inclusivity.

articles.headline: New AI tool to enhance cybersecurity
articles.summary: A cybersecurity firm has launched a new AI-powered tool designed to detect and prevent sophisticated cyber threats. The tool aims to bolster digital defenses.

articles.headline: Global trade patterns shifting amid geopolitical tensions
articles.summary: Global trade patterns are undergoing significant shifts due to ongoing geopolitical tensions and supply chain disruptions. Countries are re-evaluating trade relationships and diversification strategies.

articles.headline: National infrastructure investment plan unveiled
articles.summary: The government has detailed a comprehensive national infrastructure investment plan. The plan focuses on developing key sectors like roads, railways, and energy to boost economic growth.

articles.headline: Cancer research yields potential new treatment
articles.summary: Advances in cancer research have led to the development of a potential new treatment. Early studies show promising results in inhibiting tumor growth.

articles.headline: Tech regulations to be reviewed by parliament
articles.summary: Parliamentary committees are set to review existing technology regulations. The discussions aim to address emerging challenges in the digital space and ensure fair competition.

articles.headline: Startup ecosystem receives boost from government funding
articles.summary: The government has allocated significant funding to support the startup ecosystem. The move is expected to encourage innovation and job creation.

articles.headline: World population to reach 9 billion by 2050
articles.summary: UN projections indicate that the world population will reach approximately 9 billion by 2050. The report highlights demographic shifts and their implications.

articles.headline: Climate change impact on agriculture discussed
articles.summary: Experts discussed the growing impact of climate change on agriculture at a conference. Strategies for climate-resilient farming and sustainable land use were explored.

articles.headline: New vaccine shows promise against common virus
articles.summary: A pharmaceutical research team has developed a new vaccine showing promising efficacy against a common viral infection. Further clinical trials are planned.

articles.headline: India-US trade ties strengthen
articles.summary: Bilateral trade ties between India and the United States are strengthening, with discussions on new trade agreements and investment opportunities. Both nations aim to enhance economic cooperation.

articles.headline: Cricket team prepares for upcoming series
articles.summary: The national cricket team is undergoing rigorous preparation for its upcoming international series. Coaches are focusing on team strategy and player conditioning.

articles.headline: Astronomers detect unusual cosmic signal
articles.summary: Astronomers have detected an unusual cosmic signal from a distant galaxy. Scientists are analyzing the signal to understand its origin and nature.

articles.headline: Corporate earnings season begins with mixed results
articles.summary: The corporate earnings season has commenced with companies reporting mixed financial results. Some sectors show strong profit growth, while others face challenges.

articles.headline: RBI tightens norms for digital lending
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has introduced stricter norms for digital lending platforms. The measures aim to protect borrowers and ensure fair practices in the digital lending space.

articles.headline: New cybersecurity framework launched
articles.summary: A national cybersecurity framework has been launched to enhance digital security across critical sectors. The framework outlines measures for threat detection and response.

articles.headline: Government reviews progress on infrastructure projects
articles.summary: Senior officials reviewed the progress of key national infrastructure projects. Emphasis was placed on timely completion and efficient execution of these vital developments.

articles.headline: Global health organizations address pandemic preparedness
articles.summary: Global health organizations are discussing enhanced pandemic preparedness strategies. The focus is on strengthening surveillance, response mechanisms, and vaccine development.

articles.headline: Supreme Court issues order on environmental protection
articles.summary: The Supreme Court issued a significant order on environmental protection, directing stricter compliance with pollution control norms. The ruling impacts industrial and urban development.

articles.headline: World Bank boosts funding for climate action
articles.summary: The World Bank announced increased funding for climate action initiatives globally. The support aims to help countries transition to greener economies and adapt to climate change.

articles.headline: Indian companies expand global footprint
articles.summary: Several Indian companies are expanding their global footprint through mergers, acquisitions, and new market entries. This reflects India's growing influence in the international business landscape.

articles.headline: Congress leaders detained after dharna at PM’s house
articles.summary: Congress leaders, including Rahul Gandhi, protested at PM Narendra Modi’s residence over NEET paper leaks and were detained by Delhi Police. They were released later. Rahul Gandhi called it 'the beginning of the end of the Modi regime'.

articles.headline: Parliament fails to function on second day amid protests
articles.summary: Both Houses of Parliament were adjourned for the second consecutive day due to protests by Opposition members over the NEET paper leak and alleged Temple donation theft. Discussions on the education crisis were demanded.

articles.headline: Missing elderly woman’s body found in suitcase in lake
articles.summary: The body of a 61-year-old woman, reported missing from Kengeri last week, was found stuffed in a suitcase and dumped in Ramohalli Lake. Police have registered a murder case.

articles.headline: Civic activist passes away
articles.summary: Senior civic activist N.S. Ramakanth, 88, who advocated for sustainable waste management in Bengaluru, passed away on Monday due to age-related ailments. His eyes were donated for medical research.

articles.headline: EV tech incubation centre to be set up in Mysuru
articles.summary: Visvesvaraya Technological University (VTU) will establish an Electric Vehicle (EV) Tech Incubation Centre in Mysuru with ₹12 crore financial assistance from the State government to support EV startups.

articles.headline: KSOU to offer admissions for 65 courses
articles.summary: Karnataka State Open University (KSOU) has opened admissions for 65 courses for the 2026-27 academic year, including short-term, skill-based certificate courses, with fee exemptions for certain categories.

articles.headline: City police launch gender sensitisation course
articles.summary: Bengaluru City Police has launched an online gender sensitisation course under the Nirbhaya Project to raise awareness about gender discrimination and its harmful impacts.

articles.headline: Storage in Cauvery reservoirs stands at 52%
articles.summary: Cauvery reservoirs in Karnataka have 52% storage capacity due to deficit rainfall, particularly in Kodagu. The CWMA is meeting to review the situation, with water to be used primarily for drinking.

articles.headline: Study to evaluate impact of heat stress on health in T.N., Karnataka
articles.summary: A study to evaluate the effect of heat on physical and mental health in South India has been initiated in Tamil Nadu and Karnataka. It aims to develop a personalized heat-impact metric tool.

articles.headline: Countdown begins for expansion of Cabinet in State
articles.summary: Karnataka's Cabinet expansion is imminent, with CM D.K. Shivakumar and others consulting the high command. Around 20 legislators are expected to be inducted before the Monsoon Session.

articles.headline: HC seeks State’s response on plea seeking CBI probe into KPSC recruitments
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court has directed the State to respond to a plea seeking a CBI probe into alleged illegalities in KPSC recruitments and against suspended chairperson Shivashankar S. Sahukar.

articles.headline: Make plans to develop tourism in CRZ-notified beaches
articles.summary: Karnataka's Minister for Tourism K.J. George directed officials to assess beaches in CRZ-notified areas and prepare tourism development plans to boost beach tourism.

articles.headline: Scientists compile largest meta-dataset of tree inventory studies from India
articles.summary: A new study has compiled the largest meta-dataset of tree inventory studies from India, bringing together data from 465 published studies to understand forest dynamics.

articles.headline: New trade pact a win-win for India and U.K.
articles.summary: The India-U.K. Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) aims to double bilateral trade to over $100 billion by 2030, boosting exports, services, and investment in emerging sectors.

articles.headline: Farmers from Punjab stopped on way to Delhi to hold protest
articles.summary: Farmers from Punjab were stopped by police at inter-State boundaries while heading to Delhi for a rally against the proposed India-U.S. trade deal. They later returned after assurances.

articles.headline: Protester suffers injuries from pellet guns
articles.summary: At least one protester injured during the Parliament march sustained pellet gun injuries, marking the first known instance of security forces using such weapons in Delhi. Police deny use.

articles.headline: Thousands regroup at CJP protest site after crackdown
articles.summary: Thousands gathered at the CJP protest site in Jantar Mantar after security forces had cleared the area the previous day. They continued chants for the Education Minister's resignation.

articles.headline: From IAC to paper leaks, anti-system movements pose challenge
articles.summary: Anti-establishment movements like IAC and current protests over exam leaks challenge political parties by remaining outside the system, but often struggle to translate anger into policy once they enter governance.

articles.headline: Meghalaya murder accused must surrender, observes SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court indicated it may ask Sonam Raghuvanshi, accused of murder, to surrender unless she does so voluntarily, while key prosecution witnesses are examined.

articles.headline: Trinamool tells INDIA bloc parties to unite against BJP
articles.summary: TMC chief Mamata Banerjee urged INDIA bloc parties to unite against the BJP in West Bengal, despite her party's defeat in the Assembly elections, and announced support for the CJP protest.

articles.headline: Global wars delaying FTA negotiations
articles.summary: Ongoing global wars are delaying India's trade deal negotiations with GCC countries, Israel, and the Eurasian Economic Union, cumulatively accounting for about 20% of its total trade.

articles.headline: Indian Oil Corp said to cancel Iraq oil lifting amid attacks
articles.summary: Indian refiners IOC and Manugore Refinery have cancelled plans to lift Iraqi oil due to increased risk and attacks on vessels in the Persian Gulf and Strait of Hormuz.

articles.headline: SBI Funds lists with eye on turning leader
articles.summary: SBI Funds Management Ltd., India's largest asset manager, debuted at a 6% premium, aiming to be the market leader by expanding its distribution reach to 100 million investors.

articles.headline: ‘India-U.K. FTA gives predictability for bank firms on both sides’
articles.summary: The India-U.K. CETA creates a predictable and attractive environment for businesses, giving British firms confidence to expand in India and enhancing India's position as a global services hub.

articles.headline: Trump promises Lebanon peace with Israel
articles.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump vowed to help Lebanon pursue peace with Israel, stating the country had been 'mistreated' and 'hit hard' for decades.

articles.headline: Ed Miliband appointed Foreign Secretary
articles.summary: Ed Miliband was appointed U.K. Foreign Secretary as PM Andy Burnham formed his Cabinet, including old faces from the Keir Starmer government and new names.

articles.headline: Tehran official meets mediators in Pak. over death of Indian crew
articles.summary: An Iranian official met mediators in Pakistan amid renewed fighting, seeking to salvage a deal. Iran also hit U.S.-allied countries, while Jordan and Bahrain sounded missile alerts.

articles.headline: U.S. accuses Cuba of spy campaign, aiding ‘left-wing terrorism’
articles.summary: A U.S. State Department report accused Cuban spies of infiltrating U.S. government and aiding 'left-wing terrorism' on American soil, escalating tensions.

articles.headline: Strike on Ukraine-bound gas tanker wounds three seafarers
articles.summary: A strike on a gas tanker in the Black Sea wounded three seafarers and forced evacuation. Ukraine blamed Russia for the 'deliberate act of terror'.

articles.headline: World champion Spain supplants Argentina atop FIFA rankings
articles.summary: World champion Spain has replaced Argentina at the top of the FIFA rankings, followed by France and England. Brazil and Morocco also climbed higher.

articles.headline: FIFA to investigate players’ clash after World Cup final
articles.summary: FIFA has opened an investigation into a brief player clash on the field after the World Cup final, citing potential breaches of its disciplinary code.

articles.headline: Defending his troop just reward, says Fernandez
articles.summary: Enzo Fernandez insists Argentina represented itself in the 'best possible way' at the World Cup, emphasizing 'something bigger than just a result' after a red card and ugly incidents.

articles.headline: Spain refines its style, yapıyor winning spree
articles.summary: Spain won its second World Cup by combining its traditional possession-based style with renewed fluidity and a strong defence under coach Luis de la Fuente, outclassing opponents like Argentina and France.

articles.headline: Pranesh bounces back to stun Gukesh
articles.summary: M. Pranesh defeated reigning World champion D. Gukesh in the sixth round of the Chennai Grandmasters chess tournament, marking his best career win.

articles.headline: India U-19 bowlers put India on top
articles.summary: Indian U-19 bowlers took control on the second day of the Youth 'Test' in Colombo, reducing Sri Lanka U-19 to 122 for eight after scoring 411 runs.

articles.headline: BCCI makes changes to domestic season playing conditions
articles.summary: The BCCI has introduced new playing conditions for the 2026-27 domestic season, including bowler suspension for deliberate no-balls and wicketkeepers' gloves behind stumps.
