articles.headline: Good chance to win two medals in CWG, says Sreeshankar
articles.summary: Long jumper M. Sreeshankar believes he has a good chance to win two medals at the Commonwealth Games, having trained in Poland to acclimatize to the conditions.

articles.headline: India’s rarest heron faces a silent end
articles.summary: The critically endangered white-bellied heron, with only 6-9 individuals in India, faces local extinction due to a 1,200-MW hydroelectric project on the Lohit River, ignoring its habitat needs.

articles.headline: Beta Pictoris b: hide and seek
articles.summary: Astronomers discovered a faint, elusive planet orbiting the star Beta Pictoris after a decade of searching. It's the dimmest planet ever directly imaged from Earth.

articles.headline: India’s ICI gets an updated series
articles.summary: The government released an updated Index of Core Industries (ICI) series with a revised base year and sector weights, aiming for better reflection of industrial activity.

articles.headline: What past patterns and current concerns indicate about upcoming bypolls
articles.summary: Evidence suggests ruling parties have an advantage in byelections, but upcoming polls in Bihar and Madhya Pradesh are drawing attention due to internal party issues and candidate changes.

articles.headline: Is Yashwant Varma still a judge?
articles.summary: The resignation of former judge Yashwant Varma, facing misbehaviour charges, raises questions about his current judicial status and the accountability framework for judges.

articles.headline: Maruti Suzuki to Hike Vehicle Prices by Up to ₹30,000 from August
articles.summary: Maruti Suzuki India announced a price increase of up to ₹30,000 on its vehicle models starting August 2026, citing rising raw material and production costs. The actual hike will vary by model and variant. This is the second price revision by the carmaker this calendar year.

articles.headline: TVS Motor Q1 Profit Up 11%, Rallis India Profit Jumps 31%
articles.summary: TVS Motor reported an 11% rise in Q1 FY27 net profit to ₹1,036 crore, with revenue up 33.5%. This was driven by strong two- and three-wheeler sales. Separately, Rallis India saw a 31% Q1 net profit increase to ₹125 crore on steady demand.

articles.headline: Adani Total Gas Q1 Net Profit Falls 18% Amid Rising Gas Prices
articles.summary: Adani Total Gas (ATGL) reported an 18% drop in Q1 FY27 net profit to ₹133 crore, attributed to higher LNG procurement costs due to the West Asia conflict, despite a 27% revenue increase. ATGL is a JV with TotalEnergies.

articles.headline: 20 Killed in Sikkim Tunnel Collapse Due to Suspected Methane Gas
articles.summary: At least 20 workers died and five remain trapped after suspected methane gas release in an under-construction tunnel at NHPC's Teesta Stage-VI hydroelectric project in Sikkim's Namchi district. Rescue operations are ongoing. CM announced compensation.

articles.headline: Shiv Sena UBT Challenges Speaker's Decision on MPs' Merger in SC
articles.summary: Shiv Sena (UBT) has moved the Supreme Court challenging Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla's decision to recognize the merger of six party MPs with the Eknath Shinde faction, arguing it violates anti-defection laws. The SC will consider an urgent hearing.

articles.headline: India Summons Russian Envoy Over Deaths of Four Indian Sailors
articles.summary: India summoned Russia's Charge d'Affaires to protest the deaths of four Indian sailors aboard the MV Golden Nellio, reportedly hit by a Russian missile in the Black Sea. India demanded an investigation and safety assurances.

articles.headline: Opposition Joins Protesters at Jantar Mantar Over NEET, UGC-NET Exam Leaks
articles.summary: Opposition leaders, including Rahul Gandhi, joined student protests at Jantar Mantar demanding Union Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over NEET/UGC-NET exam irregularities. Protesters attempting to march to PM's residence were detained.

articles.headline: Trump Imposes 50% Tariffs on Canadian Imports, Sparking Trade War Fears
articles.summary: President-elect Donald Trump announced 50% tariffs on Canadian goods, including energy and autos, citing unfair practices. Canada plans retaliation, warning of a trade war and impacts on North American supply chains.

articles.headline: SC Orders Fresh Trial in 1996 Blast Case for Lack of Legal Aid
articles.summary: The Supreme Court set aside the conviction and death sentence of an accused in a 1996 blast case, ordering a fresh trial due to the violation of the right to a fair trial without adequate legal assistance.

articles.headline: SIT Formed to Probe Land Grab Allegations Against Art of Living
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has formed a Special Investigation Team (SIT) to probe allegations of the Art of Living Foundation occupying over 200 acres of government land near Bengaluru. An 11-member SIT will report within three months.

articles.headline: Three Arrested for Abduction and Rape of Minor in Bengaluru
articles.summary: Bengaluru police arrested three individuals for the alleged abduction and gang-rape of a 16-year-old girl in Devanahalli. The suspects face charges under POCSO and BNS.

articles.headline: Karnataka Cabinet Expansion Nears Finalization: Congress
articles.summary: The Congress party indicated that the Karnataka cabinet expansion is in its final stages, with CM Siddaramaiah and DyCM D.K. Shivakumar meeting the high command in Delhi to finalize ministerial appointments.

articles.headline: Activist Sonam Wangchuk Moved to Medanta Hospital After HC Order
articles.summary: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk, on hunger strike for Ladakh's statehood, was moved from Safdarjung to Medanta Hospital following a Delhi High Court order to ensure specialized medical care amid concerns over his declining health.

articles.headline: MP Assembly Passes Uniform Civil Code Bill Amid Opposition Walkout
articles.summary: Madhya Pradesh Assembly passed the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) Bill, mandating live-in relationship registration and ensuring equal inheritance rights for women. The bill was passed by voice vote amidst Congress protests.

articles.headline: Tribunal Overturns Most FEMA Charges Against Lalit Modi, BCCI
articles.summary: An appellate tribunal largely overturned Enforcement Directorate penalties against Lalit Modi and the BCCI in a FEMA case related to the 2009 IPL shift to South Africa, upholding findings on two specific counts.

articles.headline: France Backs Social Media Ban for Under-15s Without Parental Consent
articles.summary: France's National Assembly voted for a bill banning children under 15 from social media without parental consent, requiring age verification to protect minors from online harm.

articles.headline: ED Uncovers ₹300 Crore Cryptocurrency Fraud in Karnataka
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) exposed a ₹300 crore cryptocurrency scam involving self-styled influencers who allegedly defrauded foreign investors. Assets worth ₹35 million are frozen as the money-laundering probe continues.

articles.headline: Power Breakdown Halts Namma Metro Green Line for 45 Minutes
articles.summary: Namma Metro's Green Line services in Bengaluru were disrupted for 45 minutes during peak morning hours due to a power failure between Yeshwantpur and Madavara, affecting hundreds of commuters.

articles.headline: VTU Partners with Google for AI Lessons in Curriculum
articles.summary: Visvesvaraya Technological University (VTU) signed an MoU with Google to integrate AI, cloud computing, and data science into its curriculum for over 3 lakh students, enhancing employability.

articles.headline: 10 Held After Footpath Clearance Drive Turns Violent in Bengaluru
articles.summary: A civic body's footpath clearance drive near Shivajinagar bus stand turned violent, leading to the arrest of 10 individuals for allegedly attacking officials and causing injury.

articles.headline: Bengaluru-Mysuru Highway Toll Hiked Again After Three Months
articles.summary: The NHAI has again increased toll charges at Kaniminike and Sheshagirihalli plazas on the Bengaluru-Mysuru highway, less than four months after the previous revision.
